content:
🔥 nvitop | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Monitoring and managing NVIDIA GPU processes and resources in real-time from the command line.
✨ Key Features: • Interactive NVIDIA-GPU process viewer • Real-time monitoring of GPU utilization, memory, temperature, power, etc. • Process management (viewing, killing) • CUDA visible devices selection tool • Prometheus exporter for Grafana dashboards
📖 Summary: nvitop is an interactive command-line tool for monitoring and managing NVIDIA GPU processes. It provides real-time insights into GPU utilization, memory usage, and running processes, similar to `htop` for CPUs, and includes features for CUDA visible devices selection and Prometheus exporting.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 pywalium | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: To dynamically theme the Chromium browser with colors generated by Pywal, ensuring a consistent color scheme across the user's operating system and browser.
✨ Key Features: • Generates Chromium themes using Pywal colors • Automates theme creation via a shell script • Easy integration with Chromium's developer mode • Requires re-execution on Pywal color changes
📖 Summary: Pywalium is a shell script that generates custom themes for the Chromium browser based on colors defined by the Pywal utility. It automates the process of applying dynamic color schemes to Chromium, allowing users to easily match their browser's appearance to their system's wallpaper or Pywal color palette.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 TUI_bluetooth | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Managing and interacting with Bluetooth devices from a Linux terminal with a rich TUI.
✨ Key Features: • Device listing with status (connected, paired, nearby), signal strength, and battery level • Adapter control (power, visibility, scanning) • Pairing and connection with PIN/passkey handling • AVRCP media control for audio devices • Automatic audio routing via PulseAudio/PipeWire
📖 Summary: bluetui is a terminal-based TUI manager for Bluetooth devices on Linux. It provides a user-friendly interface to control adapters, manage paired and nearby devices, and interact with audio and TV remotes, all without requiring sudo privileges.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 VidBee | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Downloading videos and audio content from a wide variety of online sources through a desktop application.
✨ Key Features: • Download videos and audio from 1000+ websites worldwide • Built with Electron and powered by yt-dlp • Modern, clean, and intuitive user interface • One-click pause/resume/retry functionality
📖 Summary: VidBee is an open-source video downloader built with Electron and powered by yt-dlp. It allows users to download videos and audio from over 1000 websites worldwide through an intuitive graphical interface. Key features include support for numerous platforms, a user-friendly download queue, and an RSS auto-download function for background updates.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 dexter | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automating and enhancing deep financial research by providing data-backed answers to complex queries.
✨ Key Features: • Intelligent Task Planning • Autonomous Execution • Self-Validation • Real-Time Financial Data Access • Safety Features (loop detection, step limits)
📖 Summary: Dexter is an autonomous agent designed for deep financial research. It intelligently plans, executes, and refines research tasks using real-time market data and self-validation to provide confident, data-backed answers to complex financial questions.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
